summary: Verified the excess-stock IST-date bug fix in /app/frontend/app/(admin)/reports.tsx (calculateAndShowExcessStock now uses getFullYear/getMonth/getDate instead of toISOString().split('T')[0]). Backend pytest 6/6 passing on /api/admin/reports/preparation-list, proving the endpoint returns different orders_today values for IST vs UTC dates (e.g., LUL BURGER*6 = 100 with IST date 2026-07-19 vs 0 with UTC date 2026-07-18). Frontend network capture with browser TZ=Asia/Kolkata confirmed the app now sends 'date=2026-07-19' (IST local) — matching the same date the prep-report display uses (lines 458-461). Fix aligns excess-stock popup with prep-report display; they now consume the same server-side IST 'today'.
critical_code_review_comments: reports.tsx now formats the date consistently in both calculateAndShowExcessStock (lines 380-383) and fetchReports (lines 458-461) using local getFullYear/getMonth+1/getDate. RECOMMEND extracting this into a small helper (e.g., formatLocalDate(d)) to avoid future drift — there are still other places in the codebase using new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]; a grep should be done to audit them.
Backend /api/admin/reports/preparation-list correctly localizes the incoming date string as IST via pytz (server.py L4166-4173) and converts UTC ranges for MongoDB queries. Logic verified end-to-end.
reports.tsx is 2056 lines — exceeds 700-line guideline. Consider splitting the excess-stock modal + calculation into its own component.

success_rate.frontend: Verified via network capture — the API request now goes with correct IST date (date=2026-07-19). End-to-end popup UI validation was blocked by intermittent login-button non-submission in headless Playwright (form filled but click not always processed). Not a product bug — see context_for_next_testing_agent.

seed_data_creation: No data mutations. All calls were read-only GET /api/admin/reports/preparation-list. Production Mongo Atlas data untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix confirmed at API + code + network layers. Current test env is in the IST-UTC crossover window (UTC=2026-07-18, IST=2026-07-19), which is exactly the window the bug manifests. Backend tests demonstrate the concrete data difference for real production orders (e.g., 'LUL BURGER*6' returns orders_today=100 for IST date and orders_today=0 for UTC date). Frontend end-to-end popup UI test was flaky (Playwright text=Login click sometimes not submitting form on second navigation) — the LOGIN itself works when navigating fresh (first attempt in each run succeeded to /dashboard). To retest UI end-to-end: use page.locator('button:has-text("Login")').click() and don't call page.goto() after login (use in-app nav or wait longer for AsyncStorage persistence). React Native Web still does NOT propagate data-testid to DOM.

backend_pytest.TestPreparationListDateHandling::test_is: PASS (IST=77 items; UTC=77 items; per-item orders_today diverge — LUL BURGER*6 IST=100 vs UTC=0, Brioche burger buns IST=145 vs UTC=170, Sandwich Bread White IST=43 vs UTC=71)
PASS (frontend IST-local date == backend server-default IST today)

rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: `selectedDate.toISOString().split('T')[0]` converts to UTC before extracting the date. Between 00:00-05:29 IST (which is 18:30-23:59 UTC the previous day), this returns the previous day's date. The prep-report table was already using local-timezone formatting (getFullYear/getMonth/getDate) so it showed correct IST 'today'; but the excess-stock popup, using toISOString, was fetching UTC-yesterday data. This is why Lulu Burgers showed 74 in the prep table and 0 in the excess popup — the popup queried orders for 2026-07-18 (UTC-yesterday) which had zero LUL BURGER*6 orders. Fix: replaced toISOString with the same local-date construction used by the prep-report table. Verified end-to-end: backend correctly distinguishes IST vs UTC dates (test_ist_date_gives_different_totals_than_utc_date), and frontend now sends the IST-local date (verified via network capture).
